root_text.ud2.6:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud2.6:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa paribbājakassa daharamāṇavikā pajāpati hoti gabbhinī upavijaññā.
root_text.ud2.6:1.4: Atha kho sā paribbājikā taṁ paribbājakaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.6:1.5: “gaccha tvaṁ, brāhmaṇa, telaṁ āhara, yaṁ me vijātāya bhavissatī”ti.
root_text.ud2.6:2.1: Evaṁ vutte, so paribbājako taṁ paribbājikaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.6:2.3: Dutiyampi kho sā paribbājikā taṁ paribbājakaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.6:2.4: “gaccha tvaṁ, brāhmaṇa, telaṁ āhara, yaṁ me vijātāya bhavissatī”ti.
root_text.ud2.6:2.5: Dutiyampi kho so paribbājako taṁ paribbājikaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.6:2.7: Tatiyampi kho sā paribbājikā taṁ paribbājakaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.6:2.8: “gaccha tvaṁ, brāhmaṇa, telaṁ āhara, yaṁ me vijātāya bhavissatī”ti.
root_text.ud2.6:3.1: Tena kho pana samayena rañño pasenadissa kosalassa koṭṭhāgāre samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā sappissa vā telassa vā yāvadatthaṁ pātuṁ dīyati, no nīharituṁ.
root_text.ud2.6:4.2: “rañño kho pana pasenadissa kosalassa koṭṭhāgāre samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā sappissa vā telassa vā yāvadatthaṁ pātuṁ dīyati, no nīharituṁ.
root_text.ud2.6:4.3: Yannūnāhaṁ rañño pasenadissa kosalassa koṭṭhāgāraṁ gantvā telassa yāvadatthaṁ pivitvā gharaṁ āgantvā ucchadditvāna dadeyyaṁ, yaṁ imissā vijātāya bhavissatī”ti.
root_text.ud2.6:5.1: Atha kho so paribbājako rañño pasenadissa kosalassa koṭṭhāgāraṁ gantvā telassa yāvadatthaṁ pivitvā gharaṁ āgantvā neva sakkoti uddhaṁ kātuṁ, na pana adho.
root_text.ud2.6:5.2: So dukkhāhi tibbāhi kharāhi kaṭukāhi vedanāhi phuṭṭho āvaṭṭati parivaṭṭati.
root_text.ud2.6:6.1: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.
root_text.ud2.6:6.2: Addasā kho bhagavā taṁ paribbājakaṁ dukkhāhi tibbāhi kharāhi kaṭukāhi vedanāhi phuṭṭhaṁ āvaṭṭamānaṁ parivaṭṭamānaṁ.
root_text.ud2.6:7.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud2.6:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud2.6:1.3: Now at that time a certain wanderer had a young brahmin wife who was pregnant and about to give birth.
translation_text.ud2.6:1.5: “Go, brahmin, bring oil for my delivery.”
translation_text.ud2.6:2.2: “But where, my dear, can I get oil?”
translation_text.ud2.6:2.3: For a second time, she said,
translation_text.ud2.6:2.4: “Go, brahmin, bring oil for my delivery.”
translation_text.ud2.6:2.5: For a second time, the wanderer said,
translation_text.ud2.6:2.6: “But where, my dear, can I get oil?”
translation_text.ud2.6:2.7: For a third time, she said,
translation_text.ud2.6:2.8: “Go, brahmin, bring oil for my delivery.”
translation_text.ud2.6:3.1: Now at that time ghee and oil were being given away to any ascetic or brahmin at the storehouse of King Pasenadi of Kosala. But it was only to drink there, not to take away.
translation_text.ud2.6:4.3: “Why don’t I go to the king’s storehouse, drink as much oil as I can, then come home and throw it up so it can be used for the delivery?”
translation_text.ud2.6:5.1: Then he did just that. But when he got home he was unable to either bring it up or pass it out.
translation_text.ud2.6:5.2: He rolled to and fro, suffering painful, sharp, severe, acute feelings.
translation_text.ud2.6:6.1: Then the Buddha robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, entered Sāvatthī for alms.
translation_text.ud2.6:6.2: He saw the wanderer in agony.
translation_text.ud2.6:7.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud2.6:8.1: “Oh! How happy are those with nothing!
translation_text.ud2.6:8.2: Hence knowledge masters are people with nothing.
translation_text.ud2.6:8.3: See how troubled are those with attachments,
translation_text.ud2.6:8.4: a person bound tight to people.”

variant_text.ud2.6:4.3: ucchadditvāna → uggiritvāna (bj, sya-all, pts-vp-pli1); ucchaditvā (sya-a); ucchaḍḍitvāna (mr)
